name: BCG
description: Bacillus Calmette-Guérin vaccine protects against tuberculosis (TB), a serious lung infection.
side_effects: Small lump or sore at injection site (normal, heals over months)

name: Hepatitis B (1st dose)
description: Protects against Hepatitis B virus infection that can lead to serious liver disease.
side_effects: Mild soreness at injection site, low-grade fever

name: OPV 0 (Oral Polio Vaccine)
description: Oral polio drops protect against poliomyelitis. Given as sweet-tasting drops.

name: DPT (1st dose)
description: Combined vaccine protecting against Diphtheria, Pertussis (whooping cough), and Tetanus.
side_effects: Soreness, redness at injection site; mild fever for 1-2 days

name: Hepatitis B (2nd dose)
description: Second dose of Hepatitis B vaccine to build stronger immunity.

name: IPV (Inactivated Polio Vaccine, 1st)
description: Injected polio vaccine providing stronger, longer-lasting protection against polio.

name: Hib (1st dose)
description: Haemophilus influenzae type b vaccine protects against meningitis and pneumonia.

name: PCV (1st dose)
description: Pneumococcal Conjugate Vaccine protects against pneumococcal bacteria causing ear infections and pneumonia.
side_effects: Mild fever, soreness at injection site

name: Rotavirus (1st dose)
description: Oral vaccine protecting against rotavirus, the leading cause of severe diarrhea in children.

name: DPT (2nd dose)
description: Second dose of DPT combined vaccine.
side_effects: Soreness, redness at injection site; mild fever

name: IPV (2nd dose)
description: Second dose of inactivated polio vaccine.

name: Hib (2nd dose)
description: Second dose of Hib vaccine.

name: Hepatitis B (3rd dose)
description: Third dose completes the Hepatitis B primary series.

name: Rotavirus (2nd dose)
description: Second dose of oral rotavirus vaccine.

name: DPT (3rd dose)
description: Third and final dose of the primary DPT series.

name: IPV (3rd dose)
description: Third dose of inactivated polio vaccine.

name: Hib (3rd dose)
description: Third dose of Hib vaccine completing the primary series.

name: PCV (2nd dose)
description: Second dose of pneumococcal conjugate vaccine.

name: Rotavirus (3rd dose)
description: Third and final dose of oral rotavirus vaccine.

name: Measles / MR (1st dose)
description: Measles-Rubella vaccine protects against measles and rubella (German measles).

name: JE (1st dose)
description: Japanese Encephalitis vaccine (in endemic areas). Protects against brain infection.

name: Vitamin A (1st dose)
description: Vitamin A supplementation prevents blindness and strengthens immunity.

name: DPT Booster (1st)
description: First booster dose of DPT to maintain immunity.
side_effects: Soreness at injection site, mild fever

name: MMR (2nd dose)
description: Measles-Mumps-Rubella combined vaccine second dose.

name: OPV Booster
description: Booster dose of oral polio vaccine.

name: Hep A (1st dose)
description: Hepatitis A vaccine protects against liver infection spread through contaminated food/water.

name: Varicella (1st dose)
description: Chickenpox vaccine prevents varicella-zoster virus infection.
side_effects: Mild fever, small rash at injection site

name: DPT Booster (2nd)
description: Second booster of DPT for school-entry protection.

name: OPV (5 Year Booster)
description: Final booster of oral polio vaccine at school entry.
